task.title: Fix Slack messages + DM mounts stuck write-only (zero read-down)

events.content: Root cause: messages and users/<U>/messages mounts were sync=write-only -> flip both to mirror (pear#170)

raw.question: Why is there zero read-down on Slack channel/thread/inbound-DM content?
raw.chosen: Flip messages + users/<U>/messages mounts from write-only to mirror (pear#170); activate via rebuild + restart
alternatives: poll Slack out-of-band on a timer
treat as a cloud sync-lag bug
add a separate read-only mount alongside the write mount
raw.reasoning: The messages and users/<U>/messages mounts were configured sync=write-only, so inbound content never materialized — read-down was structurally impossible, not sync lag. Flipped both to mirror (pear#170), activated by rebuild + restart (build 4f3a28e); channel/thread/inbound-DM read-down then verified live with content. CAVEAT discovered: the relayfile#262 fast-path trap — write-only sets BootstrapComplete, so on restart the bootstrap full-pull is skipped and already-missed records (e.g. DM 1780931083) are NOT backfilled; clearState is required to force a full re-pull. Also: mirror does NOT auto-delete dispatched writebacks.

retrospective.summary: Slack messages + DM mounts had sync=write-only, making read-down impossible. pear#170 flipped them to mirror (build 4f3a28e); read-down verified live. Watch the relayfile#262 fast-path trap (BootstrapComplete skips restart backfill — needs clearState) and that mirror does not auto-delete dispatched writebacks.
retrospective.approach: Check mount sync mode FIRST when read-down is totally absent (vs partial/lagging); a write-only mount is a config bug, not a sync bug.
